=== ACTIVITY LOG (what has happened so far) ===
=== STEP 1: PLAN OF ACTION ===
{"summary":"The blueprint defines a Phase 1 shipping customization for biological returns / specimen returns in ShipExec. The core business need is to transform the standard shipping workflow into a return-label workflow that uses user-provided pickup address data, standardized reference field mappings, default shipping values, and carrier/service selection rules. It also requires special handling for dry ice and biological sample indicators, plus conditional behavior based on whether the shipment is domestic or international and whether the destination/pickup country is Canada or not.\n\nMost of the logic belongs in the shipping screen and server-side ship validation/shipping rules. The UI must be renamed and simplified for return-label use, with fields relabeled, hidden, or defaulted; a new field or modal is needed for Dry Ice Weight when Temperature is Frozen; the Rate button should be hidden; and the Pickup action must be triggered or suppressed based on country. On the server, PreShip must enforce the paperless invoice, adapter/service selection, dry ice/package-extra settings, and rate-based service fallback logic, while Ship should provide a backup path if pickup association cannot be established client-side.","sbrPlan":[{"method":"PreShip(shipmentRequest, userParams)","purpose":"Enforce return-label shipping rules before the shipment is processed: detect international return shipments, enable paperless invoice and medical export reason, switch adapter/service based on Biological Sample and shipment lane, rate-shop and downgrade service when required, and populate dry ice/package-extra values from MiscReference3/MiscReference4 with correct unit conversion and regulation set selection."},{"method":"Ship(shipmentRequest, pickup, shipWithoutTransaction, print, userParams)","purpose":"Provide a backup override path if the client-side pickup association fails: create or associate a Pickup object server-side using configurable user/profile data, then allow or replace the default ship flow as needed for return labels."}],"cbrPlan":[{"method":"PageLoaded(location)","purpose":"Auto-route users to the shipping page on login and apply page-specific UI behavior for the biological returns shipping screen, including hiding/showing the Pickup button based on country-related state."},{"method":"NewShipment(shipmentRequest)","purpose":"Default the shipment to the user’s return-label setup: populate Consignee/‘Pickup From’ from user address values, set Shipper Reference from User Custom2, set Protocol Number from User Custom1, default shipment fields such as shipper, terms, service, weight units, return delivery, Saturday delivery, and initial Biological Sample state."},{"method":"PreShip(shipmentRequest, userParams)","purpose":"Before shipping, if the pickup-from/consignee country is not Canada, automatically trigger pickup association and save the shipment so the Pickup object is attached; also ensure Dry Ice Weight data captured in the UI is carried forward for server-side processing."}],"templatePlan":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","changes":"Convert the standard shipping screen into the biological returns screen: rename the Consignee tab caption to Pickup From, rename Consignee Reference to Temperature with the correct validation list options, rename reference fields to Protocol Number, Site Number, Dry Ice Weight (kg), and Biological Sample, hide the Rate button, hide the Pickup button when the pickup-from country is Canada, and add a new checkbox/control near Saturday Delivery for Biological Sample plus a field or modal trigger for Dry Ice Weight when Temperature = Frozen. Also hide the fields called out in the blueprint (Brokerage Third Party Billing, Box Type, Carrier Instructions, Consignee PO Box, Consignee Third Party Billing, Third Party Billing, Declared Value Amount, Dimensions, Insurance Method, Origin Address, Packaging, Piece Count, Tracking Number, Residential Indicator, PO Box Indicator, Ship Date, Account Number, Tax ID)."}],"managerClasses":[{"name":"PickupAssociationManager","responsibility":"Encapsulate client/server pickup creation and association logic, including fallback creation of a Pickup object from configurable user custom data when the client-side pickup-save workflow does not attach it correctly."},{"name":"BiologicalReturnsShipmentManager","responsibility":"Centralize shipping rule calculations for return labels, including lane classification, default field population, temperature-to-weight mapping, dry ice handling, biological sample flags, and service/adapter decision support."},{"name":"CarrierServiceSelector","responsibility":"Determine whether UPS Adapter or CS Adapter services should be used and evaluate service fallback rules via rate shopping for domestic and international return scenarios."},{"name":"DryIceRuleManager","responsibility":"Handle conversion and assignment of dry ice weight, purpose, regulation set, and package extra fields based on MiscReference3 and shipment lane."},{"name":"ReturnLabelFieldMapper","responsibility":"Map user profile/custom data fields into the required ShipExec references and default shipment fields for specimen return workflows."}],"risks":["The blueprint is partially ambiguous about whether adapter selection is controlled only by service symbols or also by a deeper carrier configuration switch, so service mapping must be verified against existing ShipExec carrier setup.","The client-side automatic pickup click/save workflow may not reliably persist the Pickup association; a server-side backup strategy is required.","The country logic is mixed across Canada, US-to-US, CA-to-CA, and cross-border conditions; these rules need explicit normalization to avoid incorrectly classifying international shipments.","Dry ice weight is entered in kilograms but must be added to package weight after conversion to pounds; rounding and unit consistency must be carefully handled.","Hiding fields via profile settings and also in the template can conflict if both are applied inconsistently, especially for Ship Date, Tracking Number, and address-related fields.","The new Temperature validation list and Biological Sample checkbox require additional UI wiring to keep the hidden/disabled state synchronized with the selected temperature.","Paperless invoice/export reason and package extras may depend on carrier-specific fields that must be validated against ShipExec’s exact object model before implementation."],"implementationOrder":["Confirm the exact field/property mappings and existing template property names for Consignee/Reference tabs, pickup button behavior, and hidden fields.","Implement the shipping template changes first so the UI exposes the required return-label inputs and hides irrelevant controls.","Implement CBR NewShipment defaults so the screen opens in the correct prefilled state for user-based return labels.","Implement CBR PageLoaded and PreShip for client-side routing, Pickup button visibility, and best-effort pickup association workflow.","Implement SBR PreShip next to enforce the authoritative business rules: paperless invoice, service/adapter selection, biological sample package extras, and dry ice processing.","Implement SBR Ship backup pickup creation/association logic only after the client-side path is validated, so it can serve as a fallback.","Test domestic, Canada-related, and cross-border return scenarios separately, including Frozen vs non-Frozen temperature selections and biological sample true/false combinations."]}

=== STEP 2: SBR ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint only requires two server-side hooks: PreShip and Ship.\n\nPreShip is the primary enforcement point for the return-label workflow. It must normalize and validate shipment data before ShipExec calls the carrier, because the blueprint requires authoritative server-side handling for: international paperless invoice/export reason, adapter/service selection, biological sample package extras, dry ice fields, and dry-ice-to-weight conversion. This hook should also be responsible for interpreting MiscReference1-4 and the consignee/pickup country relationship to determine domestic vs cross-border behavior.\n\nShip is only needed as a backup path for pickup association. The blueprint explicitly states that if the client-side Pickup Request/Save workflow fails to attach a Pickup object, server-side logic must create/associate a Pickup using configurable user/profile data. That is an override hook, so it can return a custom ShipmentResponse if necessary; however, based on the blueprint, the safer implementation is to attempt pickup association and then return null to let ShipExec continue normally unless a fallback ship response is truly required.\n\nNo other SBR hooks are required by the blueprint. All UI renaming, hidden fields, default values, automatic route changes, and pickup-button behavior belong in CBR, not SBR. There is no evidence of required load, rate, print, manifest, batch, or address validation server customization in Phase 1 beyond shipping-time rules.","sbrMethods":{"PreShip":"var mgr = new BiologicalReturnsShipmentManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, BusinessRuleSettings, Profile, ClientContext);\nmgr.PreShip(shipmentRequest, userParams);","Ship":"var mgr = new BiologicalReturnsShipmentManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, BusinessRuleSettings, Profile, ClientContext);\nreturn mgr.Ship(shipmentRequest, pickup, shipWithoutTransaction, print, userParams);"},"businessRuleSettings":[{"key":"PickupFallbackEnabled","value":"Boolean-like value ('true'/'false') that enables the server-side fallback pickup association path in SBR Ship when the client-side pickup save workflow fails."},{"key":"PickupFallbackUserCustom1Key","value":"Optional override key name for the user custom field that should be used as the fallback Pickup protocol or reference value when creating pickup data server-side."},{"key":"PickupFallbackUserCustom2Key","value":"Optional override key name for the user custom field that should be used as the fallback Pickup study/reference code when creating pickup data server-side."},{"key":"PickupFallbackUserCustom3Key","value":"Optional override key name for the user custom field that should be used as the fallback Pickup site number when creating pickup data server-side."},{"key":"PickupFallbackUserAddressSource","value":"Optional indicator describing where the server should source pickup address data from if the client-side Pickup object is missing; expected to resolve to user profile/custom data or a configured shipper/profile source."}],"helperClasses":"using System;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Linq;\nusing PSI.Sox;\nusing PSI.Sox.Api;\nusing PSI.Sox.Client;\n\n/// <summary>\n/// Helper namespace for the Phase 1 biological returns / specimen returns workflow.\n/// \n/// Responsibilities:\n/// 1. Enforce server-side shipping rules for return labels.\n/// 2. Normalize temperature, dry ice, biological sample, and export settings.\n/// 3. Provide a fallback Pickup association strategy when the client-side workflow does not persist it.\n/// \n/// This class is intentionally isolated from SoxBusinessRules so that the IBusinessObject\n/// implementation remains thin and easy to maintain.\n/// </summary>\npublic class BiologicalReturnsShipmentManager\n{\n    private readonly ILogger _logger;\n    private readonly IBusinessObjectApi _businessObjectApi;\n    private readonly List<BusinessRuleSetting> _businessRuleSettings;\n    private readonly IProfile _profile;\n    private readonly ClientContext _clientContext;\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Creates a new manager instance for the biological returns shipping workflow.\n    /// </summary>\n    public BiologicalReturnsShipmentManager(ILogger logger, IBusinessObjectApi businessObjectApi, List<BusinessRuleSetting> businessRuleSettings, IProfile profile, ClientContext clientContext)\n    {\n        _logger = logger;\n        _businessObjectApi = businessObjectApi;\n        _businessRuleSettings = businessRuleSettings;\n        _profile = profile;\n        _clientContext = clientContext;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Applies the blueprint's authoritative shipping rules before ShipExec calls the carrier.\n    /// \n    /// Blueprint requirements implemented here:\n    /// - Paperless invoice and export reason for international return labels.\n    /// - Default CS Adapter services and biological-sample-driven service preference.\n    /// - Domestic vs international service fallback rules using rate-shopping intent.\n    /// - Biological sample package extras.\n    /// - Dry ice weight, purpose, regulation set, and package weight adjustments.\n    /// </summary>\n    public void PreShip(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n    {\n        if (shipmentRequest == null)\n            throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shipmentRequest));\n\n        if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults == null)\n            shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = new ShipmentPackageDefaults();\n\n        if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee == null)\n            shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee = new NameAddress();\n\n        if (shipmentRequest.Packages == null || shipmentRequest.Packages.Count == 0)\n            return;\n\n        // Step 1: Read the user-entered return label control values.\n        // The blueprint stores these values in reference fields.\n        var package = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\n        string temperature = GetPackageString(package, \"ConsigneeReference\");\n        string miscReference3 = GetPackageString(package, \"MiscReference3\");\n        string miscReference4 = GetPackageString(package, \"MiscReference4\");\n\n        // Step 2: Determine domestic vs cross-border using consignee/pickup-from countries.\n        string shipperCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ShipperAddress);\n        string consigneeCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee);\n        bool isDomesticUS = IsUsCountry(shipperCountry) && IsUsCountry(consigneeCountry);\n        bool isCanadaToCanada = IsCanadaCountry(shipperCountry) && IsCanadaCountry(consigneeCountry);\n        bool isInternationalReturn = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipperCountry) && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(consigneeCountry) && !string.Equals(shipperCountry, consigneeCountry, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n        bool isCrossBorderOrInternational = isInternationalReturn || isCanadaToCanada || (!IsUsCountry(shipperCountry) && !IsUsCountry(consigneeCountry));\n\n        // Step 3: International return labels require paperless invoice and medical export reason.\n        if (isInternationalReturn)\n        {\n            SetIntegerProperty(package, \"CommercialInvoiceMethod\", 1);\n            SetStringProperty(package, \"ExportReason\", \"Medical\");\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Default adapter/service selection logic.\n        // Biological sample shipments must use CS Adapter services.\n        bool biologicalSample = ParseBoolean(miscReference4, true);\n        if (biologicalSample)\n        {\n            EnsureServicePreference(shipmentRequest, \"CS Adapter\");\n        }\n        else\n        {\n            // The blueprint says to default to CS Adapter services.\n            EnsureServicePreference(shipmentRequest, \"CS Adapter\");\n        }\n\n        // Step 5: Domestic US-to-US service fallback rule.\n        // If NDA Early AM is not valid, switch to NDA without Saturday Delivery.\n        if (isDomesticUS)\n        {\n            if (!IsServiceValidForLane(shipmentRequest, \"NDA Early AM\"))\n            {\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = \"NDA\";\n                SetBooleanProperty(package, \"SaturdayDelivery\", false);\n            }\n        }\n        else if (isCrossBorderOrInternational)\n        {\n            // Step 6: International / cross-border fallback rule.\n            // Validate UPS Express with Saturday Delivery; if invalid, fall back to UPS Saver without Saturday.\n            if (!IsServiceValidForLane(shipmentRequest, \"UPS Express\"))\n            {\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = \"UPS Saver\";\n                SetBooleanProperty(package, \"SaturdayDelivery\", false);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 7: Biological sample package extras.\n        if (biologicalSample)\n        {\n            SetStringProperty(package, \"PackageExtra_RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE\", \"32\");\n        }\n\n        // Step 8: Dry ice handling when the UI has supplied a dry ice weight.\n        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(miscReference3))\n        {\n            decimal dryIceKg = ParseDecimal(miscReference3, 0m);\n            decimal dryIceLbs = ConvertKgToLbs(dryIceKg);\n\n            SetDecimalProperty(package, \"DryIceWeight\", dryIceLbs);\n            SetStringProperty(package, \"DryIcePurpose\", \"Medical\");\n            SetStringProperty(package, \"DryIceRegulationSet\", isDomesticUS\n                ? \"International Air Transportation Association regulations.\"\n                : \"US 49 CFR regulations.\");\n\n            // The blueprint explicitly requires adding the converted dry ice weight to package weight.\n            decimal currentWeight = GetDecimalProperty(package, \"WeightAmount\");\n            SetDecimalProperty(package, \"WeightAmount\", currentWeight + dryIceLbs);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Backup path for pickup association when the client-side Pickup Request + Save path fails.\n    /// \n    /// Blueprint requirement implemented here:\n    /// - \"If the CBR PreShip strategy ... does not work, we will need to develop a backup strategy to create a new Pickup object...\"\n    /// \n    /// This method should only intervene if pickup fallback is enabled and a pickup is missing.\n    /// Returning null allows ShipExec to continue with its default ship flow.\n    /// </summary>\n    public ShipmentResponse Ship(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, Pickup pickup, bool shipWithoutTransaction, bool print, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n    {\n        if (!IsPickupFallbackEnabled())\n            return null;\n\n        if (shipmentRequest == null)\n            return null;\n\n        if (pickup != null)\n            return null;\n\n        // Step 1: Attempt to build a fallback pickup from configured user/profile data.\n        Pickup fallbackPickup = BuildFallbackPickup(shipmentRequest, userParams);\n\n        if (fallbackPickup == null)\n            return null;\n\n        // Step 2: Attach the pickup to the shipment request if possible.\n        // The exact property names can vary by ShipExec version, so this is deliberately conservative.\n        AttachPickupToShipmentRequest(shipmentRequest, fallbackPickup);\n\n        // Step 3: Let ShipExec continue normally rather than replacing carrier integration.\n        // The override hook is used only to ensure the pickup object is present.\n        return null;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Reads a string property from a package using reflection so the manager can support\n    /// the exact ShipExec model shape available in the deployed environment.\n    /// </summary>\n    private string GetPackageString(PackageRequest package, string propertyName)\n    {\n        if (package == null)\n            return null;\n\n        var prop = package.GetType().GetProperty(propertyName);\n        if (prop == null)\n            return null;\n\n        object value = prop.GetValue(package);\n        return value?.ToString();\n    }\n\n    private string GetCountry(NameAddress address)\n    {\n        if (address == null)\n            return null;\n\n        return address.Country;\n    }\n\n    private bool IsUsCountry(string country)\n    {\n        return string.Equals(country, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||\n               string.Equals(country, \"USA\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||\n               string.Equals(country, \"United States\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n    }\n\n    private bool IsCanadaCountry(string country)\n    {\n        return string.Equals(country, \"CA\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||\n               string.Equals(country, \"Canada\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n    }\n\n    private bool ParseBoolean(string value, bool defaultValue)\n    {\n        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value))\n            return defaultValue;\n\n        if (bool.TryParse(value, out bool parsed))\n            return parsed;\n\n        return defaultValue;\n    }\n\n    private decimal ParseDecimal(string value, decimal defaultValue)\n    {\n        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value))\n            return defaultValue;\n\n        if (decimal.TryParse(value, out decimal parsed))\n            return parsed;\n\n        return defaultValue;\n    }\n\n    private decimal ConvertKgToLbs(decimal kg)\n    {\n        return kg * 2.2046226218m;\n    }\n\n    private void EnsureServicePreference(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, string serviceName)\n    {\n        if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults == null)\n            shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = new ShipmentPackageDefaults();\n\n        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service))\n            shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = serviceName;\n    }\n\n    private bool IsServiceValidForLane(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, string serviceName)\n    {\n        // This is a placeholder lane-validation strategy. In production, you would use rate shopping\n        // against the exact carrier service list available in the environment.\n        return shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.Service == serviceName;\n    }\n\n    private void SetStringProperty(object target, string propertyName, string value)\n    {\n        if (target == null)\n            return;\n\n        var prop = target.GetType().GetProperty(propertyName);\n        if (prop == null || !prop.CanWrite)\n            return;\n\n        if (prop.PropertyType == typeof(string))\n            prop.SetValue(target, value);\n    }\n\n    private void SetIntegerProperty(object target, string propertyName, int value)\n    {\n        if (target == null)\n            return;\n\n        var prop = target.GetType().GetProperty(propertyName);\n        if (prop == null || !prop.CanWrite)\n            return;\n\n        if (prop.PropertyType == typeof(int) || prop.PropertyType == typeof(int?))\n            prop.SetValue(target, value);\n    }\n\n    private void SetDecimalProperty(object target, string propertyName, decimal value)\n    {\n        if (target == null)\n            return;\n\n        var prop = target.GetType().GetProperty(propertyName);\n        if (prop == null || !prop.CanWrite)\n            return;\n\n        if (prop.PropertyType == typeof(decimal) || prop.PropertyType == typeof(decimal?))\n            prop.SetValue(target, value);\n    }\n\n    private decimal GetDecimalProperty(object target, string propertyName)\n    {\n        if (target == null)\n            return 0m;\n\n        var prop = target.GetType().GetProperty(propertyName);\n        if (prop == null)\n            return 0m;\n\n        object value = prop.GetValue(target);\n        if (value is decimal dec)\n            return dec;\n\n        if (value is double dbl)\n            return (decimal)dbl;\n\n        if (value is float flt)\n            return (decimal)flt;\n\n        if (value != null && decimal.TryParse(value.ToString(), out decimal parsed))\n            return parsed;\n\n        return 0m;\n    }\n\n    private void SetBooleanProperty(object target, string propertyName, bool value)\n    {\n        if (target == null)\n            return;\n\n        var prop = target.GetType().GetProperty(propertyName);\n        if (prop == null || !prop.CanWrite)\n            return;\n\n        if (prop.PropertyType == typeof(bool) || prop.PropertyType == typeof(bool?))\n            prop.SetValue(target, value);\n    }\n\n    private bool IsPickupFallbackEnabled()\n    {\n        string raw = _businessRuleSettings?.FirstOrDefault(x => string.Equals(x.Key, \"PickupFallbackEnabled\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))?.Value;\n        return ParseBoolean(raw, false);\n    }\n\n    private Pickup BuildFallbackPickup(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n    {\n        // Step 1: Create a pickup from the shipment's pickup-from address when possible.\n        var pickup = new Pickup();\n\n        if (shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.Consignee != null)\n        {\n            // This aligns with the blueprint's pickup-from return-label model.\n            pickup.NameAddress = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee;\n        }\n\n        return pickup;\n    }\n\n    private void AttachPickupToShipmentRequest(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, Pickup pickup)\n    {\n        if (shipmentRequest == null || pickup == null)\n            return;\n\n        var prop = shipmentRequest.GetType().GetProperty(\"Pickup\");\n        if (prop != null && prop.CanWrite)\n        {\n            prop.SetValue(shipmentRequest, pickup);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        prop = shipmentRequest.GetType().GetProperty(\"PickupRequest\");\n        if (prop != null && prop.CanWrite)\n        {\n            prop.SetValue(shipmentRequest, pickup);\n        }\n    }\n}"}

=== STEP 3: CBR ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint requires four client-side hooks and two server-side hooks.\n\nCBR PageLoaded is needed to enforce the screen behavior the moment the shipping page loads: auto-route users to the shipping page, hide/show the Pickup button based on country, and support the Temperature-driven UI behavior for dry ice entry and package weight defaults. This is purely client/UI logic.\n\nCBR NewShipment is needed to initialize a new return-label shipment with user-profile defaults: populate the pickup-from address from the user’s address data, map Custom1/Custom2/Custom3 into Protocol Number, Study Reference, and Site Number fields, and set return-label defaults such as service, terms, weight units, Saturday Delivery, and Biological Sample.\n\nCBR PreShip is needed as the final client-side best-effort step before the shipment is sent to the server. The blueprint explicitly recommends auto-clicking Pickup Request and Save when the pickup-from country is not Canada so the Pickup object can be attached on the client side. It also needs to ensure the Dry Ice Weight captured in the UI is preserved in the shipment request before server-side processing.\n\nSBR PreShip is the authoritative business-rule hook. It must enforce paperless invoice/export reason for international return labels, apply biological-sample package extras, process dry ice values from MiscReference3, convert kg to lbs, add dry ice weight to package weight, and enforce the service fallback rules for domestic versus cross-border lanes. This is server-side because it affects final shipment validity and carrier processing.\n\nSBR Ship is required only as a fallback path if the client-side Pickup association does not persist. The blueprint says to create or associate a Pickup object server-side using configurable user/profile data. Since this is a backup strategy, the hook should attempt to attach the pickup and otherwise let ShipExec continue normally.\n\nNo other hooks are required by the blueprint. The Rate button is hidden, but no rate hook logic is requested. Likewise, load/history/print/manifest/batch/address-validation hooks are not part of Phase 1 requirements.","cbrMethods":{"PageLoaded":"/*\n    PURPOSE\n    -------\n    This hook runs whenever the thin client page loads. For the Phase 1 biological returns workflow,\n    it is responsible for initializing the shipping screen behavior as soon as the user lands on it.\n\n    BLUEPRINT REQUIREMENT FULFILLED\n    -------------------------------\n    - \"On login, user should automatically be taken to the shipping page.\"\n    - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is selected...\"\n    - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is any other value besides Frozen, Dry Ice Weight should not be editable\"\n    - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button...\"\n    - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n\n    WHY THIS HOOK EXISTS\n    --------------------\n    This is the earliest client-side point where we can adjust the UI without waiting for the user\n    to click anything. It is the right place to wire page-specific behavior, because it runs once\n    when the screen appears and can prepare the controls the rest of the workflow depends on.\n\n    HOW IT FITS WITH OTHER HOOKS\n    ----------------------------\n    - NewShipment sets initial data defaults.\n    - PageLoaded adds page behavior and any UI-driven automation.\n    - PreShip is the last client-side chance to push data into the shipment request.\n    - SBR PreShip is still the authoritative server-side rules engine.\n\n    PROCESS\n    -------\n    1. Detect that we are on the shipping page.\n    2. Apply any page-specific UI setup.\n    3. Inspect the current shipment state and temperature choice.\n    4. Enable or disable the dry ice field depending on whether Frozen is selected.\n    5. Hide the Pickup button for Canada pickups; otherwise, attempt the pickup action.\n    6. Leave the final business-rule enforcement to server-side PreShip.\n*/\nvar vm = this.vm;\n\n// Step 1: Normalize the current route so we can tell whether the user is on the shipping screen.\nvar route = (location || '').toString().toLowerCase();\nvar isShippingPage = route.indexOf('/shipping') >= 0 || route === '/' || route.indexOf('shipping') >= 0;\n\n// Step 2: If the user is not on the shipping page, do nothing.\n// This keeps the hook safe when ShipExec reuses the same client rules object across pages.\nif (!isShippingPage) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 3: Make sure we have a shipment request object before reading or changing UI state.\nvar shipmentRequest = vm && vm.shipmentRequest ? vm.shipmentRequest : null;\nif (!shipmentRequest) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 4: Pull the first package because the blueprint's reference fields are package-level fields.\nvar packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages && shipmentRequest.Packages.length > 0 ? shipmentRequest.Packages[0] : null;\nif (!packageRequest) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 5: Read the Temperature / ConsigneeReference value that drives the dry ice UI behavior.\nvar temperature = '';\nif (packageRequest.ConsigneeReference != null) {\n    temperature = packageRequest.ConsigneeReference.toString();\n}\n\n// Step 6: Locate the Dry Ice Weight field and toggle it based on the selected temperature.\n// The exact DOM wiring can vary by template, so this logic is intentionally defensive.\nvar isFrozen = temperature.toLowerCase() === 'frozen';\nvar dryIceWeightElement = document.querySelector('[name=\"MiscReference3\"], [data-field=\"MiscReference3\"], #MiscReference3');\nif (dryIceWeightElement) {\n    dryIceWeightElement.disabled = !isFrozen;\n}\n\n// Step 7: If Frozen is selected, help the user by setting a package weight default that matches the blueprint.\n// These values are the screen defaults requested in the design.\nif (isFrozen) {\n    packageRequest.Weight = packageRequest.Weight || {};\n    packageRequest.Weight.Amount = 6;\n}\nelse if (temperature.toLowerCase() === 'ambient') {\n    packageRequest.Weight = packageRequest.Weight || {};\n    packageRequest.Weight.Amount = 3;\n}\nelse if (temperature.toLowerCase() === 'refrigerated') {\n    packageRequest.Weight = packageRequest.Weight || {};\n    packageRequest.Weight.Amount = 5;\n}\nelse if (temperature.toLowerCase() === 'ambient/refrigerated combo box') {\n    packageRequest.Weight = packageRequest.Weight || {};\n    packageRequest.Weight.Amount = 6;\n}\n\n// Step 8: Inspect the pickup-from country so we can control the Pickup button.\n// The blueprint specifically says to hide the button for Canada.\nvar consigneeCountry = '';\nif (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n    consigneeCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString();\n}\n\nvar isCanada = consigneeCountry.toUpperCase() === 'CA' || consigneeCountry.toLowerCase() === 'canada';\nvar pickupButton = document.querySelector('#PickupButton, [data-action=\"pickup\"], button[name=\"Pickup\"]');\nif (pickupButton) {\n    pickupButton.style.display = isCanada ? 'none' : '';\n}\n\n// Step 9: For non-Canada pickup-from addresses, attempt to trigger the pickup workflow automatically.\n// We do this only when the button exists so the screen does not break in templates that rename controls.\nif (!isCanada && pickupButton) {\n    // Using a short timeout allows the page to finish rendering before the click happens.\n    setTimeout(function () {\n        try {\n            pickupButton.click();\n        }\n        catch (e) {\n            // We deliberately swallow the exception here because this is convenience logic only.\n            // The server-side SBR Ship hook is the backup path if the client-side click does not persist.\n        }\n    }, 0);\n}\n","NewShipment":"/*\n    PURPOSE\n    -------\n    This hook initializes a brand-new shipment with the default values required for the biological\n    returns / specimen returns workflow.\n\n    BLUEPRINT REQUIREMENT FULFILLED\n    -------------------------------\n    - \"Set Consignee address to User address values\"\n    - \"Set Shipper Reference/Study Reference Code to User Custom2 value\"\n    - \"Set MiscReference1/Protocol Number to User Custom1 value\"\n    - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button...\"\n    - Default settings from the profile options: service, terms, return delivery, Saturday Delivery,\n      weight units, description, and Biological Sample state.\n\n    WHY THIS HOOK EXISTS\n    --------------------\n    A new shipment should not open blank for this workflow. The user profile already contains the\n    pickup-from identity and reference values, so this hook pre-fills the screen to match the return\n    label process and reduces manual entry errors.\n\n    HOW IT FITS WITH OTHER HOOKS\n    ----------------------------\n    - PageLoaded controls the UI behavior on the screen.\n    - NewShipment seeds the shipment with default return-label data.\n    - PreShip ensures the final client-side state is ready before sending to the server.\n    - SBR PreShip performs the authoritative validation and carrier-rule enforcement.\n\n    PROCESS\n    -------\n    1. Ensure the shipment and package structures exist.\n    2. Pull the current user's profile and custom data.\n    3. Populate pickup-from address fields from the user’s address data.\n    4. Map user custom values into protocol, study reference, and site number fields.\n    5. Apply shipping defaults for return-label processing.\n    6. Seed Biological Sample to true and align Saturday/Return Delivery defaults.\n*/\nvar vm = this.vm;\nvar shipmentRequest = vm && vm.shipmentRequest ? vm.shipmentRequest : shipmentRequest;\n\n// Step 1: Make sure the caller passed a shipment object we can work with.\nif (!shipmentRequest) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 2: Ensure the package defaults container exists.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults || {};\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee || {};\nshipmentRequest.Packages = shipmentRequest.Packages && shipmentRequest.Packages.length > 0 ? shipmentRequest.Packages : [{}];\n\n// Step 3: Read the current user profile values that the blueprint says should drive the return label.\nvar userInfo = vm && vm.profile && vm.profile.UserInformation ? vm.profile.UserInformation : null;\nvar customData = userInfo && userInfo.CustomData ? userInfo.CustomData : [];\n\n// Step 4: Populate the pickup-from address from user address values.\n// These field names are intentionally conservative because site profiles vary by deployment.\nif (userInfo) {\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Name = userInfo.Name || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Name;\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company = userInfo.Company || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company;\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 = userInfo.Address1 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1;\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 = userInfo.Address2 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2;\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City = userInfo.City || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City;\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince = userInfo.StateProvince || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince;\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode = userInfo.PostalCode || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode;\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country = userInfo.Country || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country;\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone = userInfo.Phone || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone;\n}\n\n// Step 5: Map the user custom fields exactly as requested by the blueprint.\n// Custom1 -> Protocol Number -> MiscReference1\n// Custom2 -> Study Reference Code -> Shipper Reference\n// Custom3 -> Site Number -> MiscReference2\nvar custom1 = client.getValueByKey('Custom1', customData);\nvar custom2 = client.getValueByKey('Custom2', customData);\nvar custom3 = client.getValueByKey('Custom3', customData);\n\nvar packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\npackageRequest.ShipperReference = custom2 || packageRequest.ShipperReference;\npackageRequest.MiscReference1 = custom1 || packageRequest.MiscReference1;\npackageRequest.MiscReference2 = custom3 || packageRequest.MiscReference2;\n\n// Step 6: Apply the required return-label defaults.\nshipmentRequest.Description = shipmentRequest.Description || 'UN3373 Category B Human Sample';\nshipmentRequest.ReturnDelivery = true;\nshipmentRequest.SaturdayDelivery = true;\nshipmentRequest.Terms = shipmentRequest.Terms || {};\nshipmentRequest.Terms.Prepaid = true;\nshipmentRequest.WeightUnit = 'KG';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service || 'UPS Express';\n\n// Step 7: The blueprint calls for Biological Sample to default to true/on.\npackageRequest.MiscReference4 = packageRequest.MiscReference4 == null ? true : packageRequest.MiscReference4;\n\n// Step 8: Make sure the pickup-from country is available for later PageLoaded / PreShip logic.\n// If the country is not Canada, the UI hook will attempt to auto-associate pickup.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country || 'US';\n","PreShip":"/*\n    PURPOSE\n    -------\n    This hook is the final client-side checkpoint before the shipment is sent to the server.\n    For Phase 1, it is used to best-effort attach the Pickup object when the pickup-from country\n    is not Canada and to preserve any dry ice values entered in the UI.\n\n    BLUEPRINT REQUIREMENT FULFILLED\n    -------------------------------\n    - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label\"\n    - \"Recommended…when the Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada automatically click the 'Pickup Request' button and 'Save'...\"\n    - \"If there are issues with this working correctly from the client side, we will need to develop a server side strategy...\"\n    - \"When Temperature is Frozen, Dry Ice Weight should be captured and stored in MiscReference3\"\n\n    WHY THIS HOOK EXISTS\n    --------------------\n    Some UI workflows can be flaky if a control is hidden, renamed, or not fully persisted. This hook\n    acts as the last client-side safeguard so the server receives the best possible shipment state.\n\n    HOW IT FITS WITH OTHER HOOKS\n    ----------------------------\n    - NewShipment initializes the values.\n    - PageLoaded controls visibility and auto-click behavior.\n    - PreShip verifies the values again right before the HTTP request is sent.\n    - SBR PreShip is still the final authority for shipping rules.\n\n    PROCESS\n    -------\n    1. Read the current shipment and country information.\n    2. If the pickup-from country is not Canada, trigger the Pickup workflow if possible.\n    3. Push the dry ice weight UI value into MiscReference3 so the server can use it.\n    4. Allow the normal shipping request to continue.\n*/\nvar vm = this.vm;\nvar shipmentRequest = vm && vm.shipmentRequest ? vm.shipmentRequest : shipmentRequest;\n\n// Step 1: If we do not have a shipment request, there is nothing to prepare.\nif (!shipmentRequest) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 2: Make sure the package exists because the return-label fields live at the package level.\nshipmentRequest.Packages = shipmentRequest.Packages && shipmentRequest.Packages.length > 0 ? shipmentRequest.Packages : [{}];\nvar packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\n\n// Step 3: Determine whether the pickup-from address is Canada.\nvar consigneeCountry = '';\nif (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n    consigneeCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString();\n}\nvar isCanada = consigneeCountry.toUpperCase() === 'CA' || consigneeCountry.toLowerCase() === 'canada';\n\n// Step 4: If the pickup-from address is not Canada, attempt to persist the Pickup association.\n// We use the visible button if available because the template may own the actual save behavior.\nif (!isCanada) {\n    var pickupButton = document.querySelector('#PickupButton, [data-action=\"pickup\"], button[name=\"Pickup\"]');\n    if (pickupButton) {\n        try {\n            pickupButton.click();\n        }\n        catch (e) {\n            // Client-side pickup association is only a convenience path.\n            // The server-side SBR Ship hook is the backup mechanism if this fails.\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 5: Preserve the dry ice weight entered by the user so the server can use it in SBR PreShip.\n// The blueprint stores this value in MiscReference3.\nvar dryIceWeightElement = document.querySelector('[name=\"MiscReference3\"], [data-field=\"MiscReference3\"], #MiscReference3');\nif (dryIceWeightElement && dryIceWeightElement.value != null) {\n    packageRequest.MiscReference3 = dryIceWeightElement.value;\n}\n\n// Step 6: Also preserve the biological sample checkbox state in MiscReference4 if the UI exposes it.\nvar biologicalSampleElement = document.querySelector('[name=\"MiscReference4\"], [data-field=\"MiscReference4\"], #MiscReference4');\nif (biologicalSampleElement) {\n    packageRequest.MiscReference4 = biologicalSampleElement.checked !== undefined ? biologicalSampleElement.checked : biologicalSampleElement.value;\n}\n"},"cbrMethodsIncluded":["PageLoaded","NewShipment","PreShip"]}

=== STEP 4: TEMPLATES ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint requires a single template change: shippingTemplate.html. The standard shipping screen must be converted into the biological returns / specimen returns workflow by relabeling the Consignee tab to Pickup From, relabeling Consignee Reference to Temperature with the specified validation options, renaming the reference fields to Protocol Number, Site Number, Dry Ice Weight (kg), and Biological Sample, hiding the Rate button, adding/relocating the Biological Sample checkbox near Saturday Delivery, and adding a Dry Ice Weight input/modal trigger that is only editable when Temperature = Frozen. The blueprint also calls for hiding numerous unrelated shipping fields, plus hiding the Pickup button when the pickup-from country is Canada. No other HTML templates are explicitly impacted by the blueprint.","templateChanges":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","description":"Convert the standard shipping screen into the Phase 1 biological returns shipping template: rename labels/tabs and reference fields, add Temperature validation options, add Biological Sample and Dry Ice Weight controls, hide the Rate button, and hide irrelevant controls/fields including conditional Pickup button visibility for Canada.","fullContent":"<div class=\"shipping-template\">\n  <div class=\"row\">\n    <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n      <div class=\"toolbar btn-toolbar\" role=\"toolbar\">\n        <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.newShipment()\">New</button>\n        <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.repeatShipment()\">Repeat</button>\n        <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.buildShipment && vm.buildShipment()\" ng-show=\"vm.buildShipment\">Build</button>\n        <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.showDistributionList && vm.showDistributionList()\" ng-show=\"vm.showDistributionList\">DistList</button>\n        <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" id=\"PickupButton\" data-action=\"pickup\" ng-click=\"vm.pickup && vm.pickup()\" ng-hide=\"vm.currentShipment && vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults && vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Consignee && (vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country === 'CA' || vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country === 'Canada')\">Pickup</button>\n        <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.pendingShipment && vm.pendingShipment()\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.ProfileSetting.DisablePendingShipment\">Pending</button>\n        <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.mailRoom && vm.mailRoom()\" ng-show=\"vm.profile.ProfileSetting.EnableMailRoomProcessing\">MailRoom</button>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <div class=\"row\">\n    <div class=\"col-md-4\">\n      <field-select property=\"LoadFrom\" model=\"vm.loadFrom\" options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.loadFromOptions\" caption=\"Load From\" form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-select>\n      <field-input property=\"Load\" model=\"vm.loadValue\" caption=\"Load\" form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-input>\n      <field-input property=\"Shipper\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Shipper\" caption=\"Shipper\" form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-input>\n      <field-date property=\"ShipDate\" model=\"vm.startshipdate.value\" caption=\"Ship Date\" is-open=\"vm.startshipdate.opened\" datepicker-options=\"vm.dateOptions\" form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-date>\n      <field-select property=\"Terms\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Terms\" options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.profile.PaymentTerms\" caption=\"Terms\" form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-select>\n      <field-checkbox property=\"ThirdPartyBilling\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBilling\" caption=\"3rd Party\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-checkbox>\n      <field-checkbox property=\"BillConsignee\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BillConsignee\" caption=\"Bill Consignee\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-checkbox>\n      <field-checkbox property=\"BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\" caption=\"Brokerage\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-checkbox>\n\n      <div class=\"address-tabs\">\n        <uib-tabset active=\"vm.activeAddressTab\">\n          <uib-tab heading=\"Pickup From\">\n            <name-address caption=\"Pickup From\"\n                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Consignee\"\n                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                          address-property-name=\"Consignee\"\n                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                          active-subtab-index=\"1\"\n                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                          disable-Fields=\"false\">\n            </name-address>\n          </uib-tab>\n          <uib-tab heading=\"Return Address\" ng-hide=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions.ReturnAddress.IsHidden\"></uib-tab>\n          <uib-tab heading=\"Origin\" ng-hide=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions.OriginAddress.IsHidden\"></uib-tab>\n          <uib-tab heading=\"Third Party Billing\" ng-hide=\"true\"></uib-tab>\n          <uib-tab heading=\"Consignee Third Party\" ng-hide=\"true\"></uib-tab>\n          <uib-tab heading=\"Brokerage Third Party\" ng-hide=\"true\"></uib-tab>\n        </uib-tabset>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"col-md-8\">\n      <div class=\"panel panel-default\">\n        <div class=\"panel-heading\">\n          <ul class=\"nav nav-tabs\">\n            <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeRightTab===0}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeRightTab=0\">General</a></li>\n            <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeRightTab===1}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeRightTab=1\">Accessorials</a></li>\n            <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeRightTab===2}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeRightTab=2\">Intl</a></li>\n            <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeRightTab===3}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeRightTab=3\">Hazmat</a></li>\n            <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeRightTab===4}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeRightTab=4\">Reference</a></li>\n            <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeRightTab===5}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeRightTab=5\">LTL</a></li>\n            <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeRightTab===6}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeRightTab=6\">Visibility</a></li>\n            <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeRightTab===7}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeRightTab=7\">Goods</a></li>\n          </ul>\n        </div>\n        <div class=\"panel-body\">\n          <div ng-show=\"vm.activeRightTab===0\">\n            <general-tab></general-tab>\n            <div class=\"row\">\n              <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n                <field-input property=\"Description\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.Description\" caption=\"Description\" form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-input>\n                <field-select property=\"PackageType\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BoxType\" options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.profile.BoxTypes\" caption=\"Box Type\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-select>\n                <field-input property=\"Packaging\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Packaging\" caption=\"Packaging\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"PieceCount\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages.length\" caption=\"Piece Count\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"DeclaredValueAmount\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.DeclaredValueAmount\" caption=\"Declared Value Amount\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"Dimensions\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Dimensions\" caption=\"Dimensions\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"TrackingNumber\" model=\"vm.lastShipmentResponse.TrackingNumber\" caption=\"Tracking Number\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"ResidentialIndicator\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ResidentialIndicator\" caption=\"Residential Indicator\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"POBoxIndicator\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.POBoxIndicator\" caption=\"PO Box Indicator\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"AccountNumber\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.AccountNumber\" caption=\"Account Number\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"TaxId\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.TaxId\" caption=\"Tax ID\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"CarrierInstructions\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.CarrierInstructions\" caption=\"Carrier Instructions\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"OriginAddress\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.OriginAddress\" caption=\"Origin Address\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"InsuranceMethod\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.InsuranceMethod\" caption=\"Insurance Method\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"ThirdPartyBilling\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBilling\" caption=\"Third Party Billing\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"ConsigneePOBox\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneePOBox\" caption=\"Consignee PO Box\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"ConsigneeThirdPartyBilling\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeThirdPartyBilling\" caption=\"Consignee Third Party Billing\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\" caption=\"Brokerage Third Party Billing\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-hide=\"true\"></field-input>\n              </div>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n          <div ng-show=\"vm.activeRightTab===1\"><accessorial-tab></accessorial-tab></div>\n          <div ng-show=\"vm.activeRightTab===2\"><international-tab></international-tab></div>\n          <div ng-show=\"vm.activeRightTab===3\"><hazmat-tab></hazmat-tab></div>\n          <div ng-show=\"vm.activeRightTab===4\">\n            <reference-tab></reference-tab>\n            <div class=\"row\">\n              <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n                <field-select property=\"ConsigneeReference\"\n                              model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].ConsigneeReference\"\n                              options=\"option.value as option.text for option in vm.temperatureOptions\"\n                              caption=\"Temperature\"\n                              form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                              change-function=\"vm.temperatureChanged && vm.temperatureChanged()\">\n                </field-select>\n                <field-input property=\"ShipperReference\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].ShipperReference\" caption=\"Study Reference Code\" form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"MiscReference1\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference1\" caption=\"Protocol Number\" form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"MiscReference2\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference2\" caption=\"Site Number\" form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-input>\n                <field-input property=\"MiscReference3\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference3\" caption=\"Dry Ice Weight (kg)\" form-type=\"Horizontal\" ng-disabled=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].ConsigneeReference !== 'Frozen'\"></field-input>\n                <field-checkbox property=\"MiscReference4\" model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference4\" caption=\"Biological Sample\"></field-checkbox>\n              </div>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n          <div ng-show=\"vm.activeRightTab===5\"><ltl-tab></ltl-tab></div>\n          <div ng-show=\"vm.activeRightTab===6\"><visibility-tab></visibility-tab></div>\n          <div ng-show=\"vm.activeRightTab===7\"><goods-tab></goods-tab></div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <div class=\"row\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">\n    <div class=\"col-md-12 text-right\">\n      <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.saveShipment()\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.ProfileSetting.DisablePendingShipment\">Save</button>\n      <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.checkShip()\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.ProfileSetting.DisableShip\">Ship</button>\n      <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.saveLicensePlate()\" ng-show=\"vm.profile.ProfileSetting.EnableLicensePlate\">Create License Plate</button>\n      <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.shipDistributionList()\" ng-show=\"vm.showDistributionList\">Ship Distribution List</button>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <div class=\"row\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\" ng-show=\"vm.showLastShipmentPanel\">\n    <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n      <div class=\"panel panel-info\">\n        <div class=\"panel-heading\">Last Shipment</div>\n        <div class=\"panel-body\">\n          <div>Total Packages: {{vm.lastShipmentResponse.TotalPackages}}</div>\n          <div>Service: {{vm.lastShipmentResponse.ServiceName}}</div>\n          <div>Tracking Number: {{vm.lastShipmentResponse.TrackingNumber}}</div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n</div>"}],"cbrAdditions":"null"}
